mission.title: dapr: Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing messaging infrastructure
mission.description: Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing messaging infrastructure. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
Currently, Dapr is overly opinionated with regard to enforcing the publishing of messages conforming to the Cloud Event specification.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing…
Upgrade dapr to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade dapr:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr
helm list -n dapr
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
IMHO, when interfacing two completely different systems, you're always going to have some sort of boundary where you have to convert from one data-format to another.

In this case, you could have a translation service that receives publishes from Dapr destined for the other service, terminating the tracing span, translating the event to what the other system recognizes, and publishing it.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
